pi_items.source: task.json description states 'You are an Infrastructure Capacity Planner...'
task.json description uses terms like 'EC2 instance' and 'datacenter thermal load', corresponding to Cloud Infrastructure.
verifier.py reads from RESULT_PATH = '/tmp/executive_capacity_report_result.json', and export_result.sh outputs to this path

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real-world time-series data from the Numenta Anomaly Benchmark (NAB) dataset. Specifically, it uses real server temperature data (machine_temperature_system_failure.csv from the realKnownCause subset) mapped to 'servers.datacenter.machine_temperature', and real EC2 CPU utilization data (ec2_cpu_utilization_5f5533.csv from the realAWSCloudwatch subset) mapped to 'servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization'. The dashboard must be named exactly 'Executive Capacity Report' and contain two graphs with exact titles: 'Compute Headroom' and 'Thermal Headroom'. The verifier strictly checks for specific function applications, including constantLine(85), constantLine(80), cactiStyle(), color(), dashed(), and alias().
